name: Hermes Agent
description: Open-source, self-improving AI agent framework with persistent memory and automated skill creation.
longDescription: Hermes Agent is an open-source, self-improving artificial intelligence (AI) agent framework developed by Nous Research, launched in February 2026. It distinguishes itself from other AI tools by continuously learning and improving through a "closed learning loop." Unlike many traditional AI tools that reset after each session, Hermes Agent learns from successfully completed tasks. It extracts reusable patterns and strategies, codified as "skills" (which are markdown documents), and stores them. This allows the agent to refine its abilities and apply past knowledge to future, similar tasks, leading to improved efficiency over time. Hermes Agent maintains persistent memory across sessions, remembering user preferences, project contexts, and environmental details. This eliminates the need for users to repeatedly provide the same information, making interactions more efficient and personalized. Released under an MIT License, Hermes Agent is an open-source framework designed to run on a user's own server (supporting Linux, macOS, and WSL2). This offers enhanced data privacy and user control over the agent's operations. When Hermes Agent successfully resolves a complex problem, it automatically generates a structured "skill document" that encapsulates the reasoning and steps taken. These skills adhere to the agentskills.io open standard, making them portable and reusable. The agent supports flexible tool chaining and includes over 40 built-in tools for various tasks, such as web search, browser automation, file manipulation, image generation, and text-to-speech. It can also integrate with multiple messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, and Signal, and is compatible with various AI models from providers like Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepSeek, and OpenRouter. Hermes Agent is suitable for a wide range of applications, including general-purpose AI assistant tasks, developing custom agent workflows, generating training data for machine learning, conducting reinforcement learning experiments, and fine-tuning AI models. It is particularly beneficial for developers, researchers, and users who require an AI system that gains cumulative value through continuous use.

name: Oracle Select AI 26ai
description: Enhanced Natural Language to SQL capabilities with feedback mechanism for improved accuracy and learning.
longDescription: The Oracle Select AI 26ai release significantly enhances Natural Language to SQL (NL2SQL) capabilities by introducing a "Feedback" mechanism, among other improvements, to refine SQL query generation. Oracle 26ai represents a fundamental shift in database architecture, integrating artificial intelligence directly into the database kernel to allow for semantic interaction with data. Key aspects include: NL2SQL allows users to interact with data using natural language prompts to generate, execute, explain, or narrate SQL queries; Feedback for NL2SQL (new in 26ai) allows users to provide feedback on SQL accuracy/relevance to build a repository of prompts and corresponding feedback for prompt tuning; Vector index for learning is automatically created when feedback is first used to identify similar prompts and provide top matching examples as additional metadata; Types of Feedback include positive feedback to confirm accuracy and negative feedback to point out errors or offer guidance; Other enhancements include automatic object selection to send only relevant table metadata, enriched prompt metadata sources, data control for administrators to limit data sent to LLM, and reduced hallucinations to prevent LLM from generating incorrect table names. Oracle Select AI 26ai aims to make database interaction more intuitive and efficient, enabling broader range of users to leverage enterprise data through natural language.

This new standalone entity aims to integrate Anthropic's Claude AI system into the core business operations of companies, specifically targeting mid-sized businesses and those within the private equity firms' portfolios. The new firm is backed by a consortium of leading alternative asset managers, including General Atlantic, Leonard Green, Apollo Global Management, GIC, and Sequoia Capital. The venture is expected to involve a total commitment of approximately $1.5 billion, with Anthropic, Blackstone, and Hellman & Friedman each anticipated to invest around $300 million, and Goldman Sachs contributing approximately $150 million. According to Krishna Rao, Chief Financial Officer of Anthropic, the demand for Claude in enterprise settings significantly surpasses any single delivery model, and this new firm will provide additional operational capabilities and capital to expand Claude's reach to large enterprises. Jon Gray, President and Chief Operating Officer of Blackstone, stated that the intention is to build a scaled, world-class company to deploy Anthropic's technology across a wide range of businesses, helping to overcome bottlenecks in enterprise AI adoption. Patrick Healy, CEO at Hellman & Friedman, highlighted the convergence of a massive market need, Anthropic's AI capabilities, and the consortium's reach to scale rapidly. The firm's engineers will work closely with Anthropic's research and product teams to design, build, and maintain enterprise AI deployments, ensuring that implementations can evolve as Claude's capabilities improve. This initiative is expected to accelerate AI adoption in various industries, including healthcare, manufacturing, financial services, retail, real estate, and infrastructure.

name: Cloudflare-Stripe Protocol for AI Agents
description: Joint protocol enabling AI agents to autonomously create accounts, purchase domains, and deploy applications without human intervention.
longDescription: As of May 2026, Cloudflare and Stripe have jointly launched a new protocol that enables AI agents to autonomously create accounts, purchase domains, and deploy applications without requiring direct human intervention for each step. This functionality represents a significant advancement in AI automation, allowing AI agents to handle entire deployment workflows from conception to a live production environment. Key aspects include: Cloudflare announced on April 30, 2026, that AI agents can now open Cloudflare accounts, acquire domain names, initiate paid subscriptions, and deploy applications to production. The system leverages a new protocol co-designed with Stripe, which acts as the identity provider. When an agent attempts to provision Cloudflare resources, Cloudflare checks for an existing account linked to the user's Stripe email. If no account exists, Cloudflare automatically provisions one and securely provides credentials to the Stripe Projects CLI for the agent's use. For existing accounts, a standard OAuth flow is used to grant access. Developers who are logged into Stripe can use a single command, `stripe projects init`, through the Stripe CLI. This command instructs an AI agent to build and deploy a new application, including the registration of a domain, without the developer needing to access the Cloudflare dashboard or input payment details directly. Stripe has expanded its "Agentic Commerce Suite" and introduced the "Machine Payments Protocol (MPP)" to facilitate programmatic transactions by AI agents. Stripe's Link wallet has also been adapted to allow agents to make payments on behalf of users, with built-in controls for spending approvals and full purchase visibility. Cloudflare has simultaneously been enhancing its "Agent Cloud" with various features, including a Registrar API that enables AI agents to register domains programmatically, and a suite of tools designed for building, deploying, and scaling AI agents on its global network.

ServiceNow Otto is designed to unify conversational AI, autonomous workflows, and enterprise search into a single experience, aiming to complete work across various systems and departments. ServiceNow highlights that many existing AI solutions excel at providing answers but often fall short in completing multi-step tasks that span different applications and teams. Otto addresses this "completion problem" by integrating intelligence from Now Assist, Moveworks, and the broader ServiceNow AI Experience. The platform is intended to act as an "AI agent of agents," allowing employees, partners, and customers to submit requests in natural language through various channels like chat, voice, mobile, or web. Otto then understands the intent, routes the work to the appropriate system or agent, and executes the task to completion, adapting to the user's role and location. Actions taken by Otto are governed by the AI Control Tower, which provides oversight, enforces policies, and offers explainability for decisions. ServiceNow's Knowledge 2026 conference had a central theme of "Put AI to Work: The Agentic Era," emphasizing the shift from assistive to agentic AI. Key platform highlights at the event included AI Agents, the AI Control Tower for governance, and RaptorDB for high-performance, enterprise-scale AI. The introduction of ServiceNow Otto underscores the company's strategy to embed AI deeply into workflows and provide a unified "front door" for AI-driven execution across the enterprise.

Users can access "gens" through tokens, which can be purchased or earned via referrals. Subscription options are also under development.

name: OpenAI GPT-Rosalind
description: OpenAI's most capable AI model specifically designed for life sciences research, accelerating drug discovery and biological research.
longDescription: OpenAI introduced GPT-Rosalind in April 2026 as its most capable AI model specifically designed for life sciences research. This specialized reasoning model aims to accelerate early-stage drug discovery, biological research, and translational medicine. GPT-Rosalind is engineered for deep scientific reasoning, enhanced use of scientific tools and databases, and safe access to multi-step workflows. Its capabilities span areas such as protein understanding, genomics analysis, biochemistry reasoning, target discovery and validation, pathway analysis, literature synthesis, and hypothesis generation. The model is particularly useful for synthesizing evidence, exploring new possibilities, identifying overlooked connections, and generating better hypotheses sooner in complex research workflows. Named after the British chemist Rosalind Franklin, whose work was crucial in revealing the structure of DNA, GPT-Rosalind is an enterprise offering available as a research preview through ChatGPT, Codex, and the API for qualified customers. OpenAI is collaborating with biopharma and research organizations like Amgen, Moderna, Thermo Fisher Scientific, and the Allen Institute to apply GPT-Rosalind across their discovery processes. This model is the first in OpenAI's life sciences series, signaling a long-term commitment to developing AI that can significantly accelerate scientific discovery in critical areas like human health. While it can streamline tasks and assist with data interpretation and experiment planning, OpenAI emphasizes that GPT-Rosalind is a reasoning tool to work alongside domain experts, not to replace them, with expert oversight being essential for validating outputs and making final research decisions.

longDescription: OpenAI launched GPT-5.5 Instant on May 5, 2026, as the new default model for ChatGPT, aiming to provide smarter, clearer, and more personalized responses while significantly reducing hallucinations. This update replaces the previous GPT-5.3 Instant model. Key improvements and features of GPT-5.5 Instant include: 52.5% reduction in hallucinated claims compared to GPT-5.3 Instant on high-stakes prompts in fields like medicine, law, and finance; decreased inaccurate claims by 37.3% in challenging conversations previously flagged for factual errors; designed to provide tighter, more direct, and concise answers without losing substance; improved personalization with better use of context from past chats, uploaded files, and connected services; enhanced capabilities in analyzing photo and image uploads, answering STEM-related questions, and deciding when to use web search for more useful answers; demonstrated gains in visual reasoning, math (AIME 2025 accuracy jumped from 65.4% to 81.2%), and science; and aims for a more natural conversational tone. For more complex tasks, ChatGPT may automatically switch to the GPT-5.5 Thinking model, which uses deeper reasoning. GPT-5.5 Instant is rolling out to all ChatGPT users, including the free tier.

name: Newforma Vojo
description: AI assistant launched May 7, 2026, designed to enhance project information management, content analysis, and task completion for AEC firms.
longDescription: Newforma Vojo, an AI assistant launched on May 7, 2026, at Newforma World 2026, is designed to enhance project information management, content analysis, and task completion for architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC) firms. Built on an agent-driven framework, Vojo aims to actively perform tasks on behalf of users, embedding intelligence directly into daily workflows. Key capabilities include: searching project information through natural language search across project data with cross-project query support; analyzing content through deep content analysis across Building Information Models (BIM) and other project documents; and completing tasks through features like AI-assisted submittal reviews to accelerate compliance checks and Smart Email Filing system that uses AI to automatically associate emails with projects and recommend filing locations for RFIs, submittals, and action items, thereby creating a more complete and defensible project record. These AI capabilities are supported by Newforma's strategic partnership with Amazon Web Services (AWS), providing a secure and scalable infrastructure for advanced AI innovation. Newforma's approach with Vojo focuses on integrating AI to surface insights, reduce manual effort, and enable more confident decision-making in the AEC industry.

name: Lovelace Elemental
description: Enterprise context engine builder designed to significantly enhance the investigative capabilities of AI agents when handling complex queries.
longDescription: Lovelace has recently emerged from stealth to introduce Elemental, an enterprise context engine builder designed to significantly enhance the investigative capabilities of AI agents when handling complex queries. The company officially launched Elemental in late April 2026. Elemental is engineered to meet the demands of speed, scale, and accuracy in high-stakes environments, claiming to increase the investigative power of AI agents by 1000 times on complex queries. This system aims to overcome challenges faced by current AI deployments, such as the lack of reliable context, which can hinder the adoption of AI agents in complex and dynamic settings. At the core of Lovelace's offering is its proprietary YottaGraph, which unifies data ingestion, entity resolution, and graph construction into a single pipeline. This process enriches the context engines with real-time intelligence, providing AI agents with the necessary contextual awareness to form fast, high-confidence conclusions. Lovelace asserts that Elemental delivers over 99.5% entity accuracy and deep-research insights at the speed and cost of a simple query, making it the only provider of enterprise-scale context engines for mission-critical applications. Founded by Andrew Moore, former head of Google Cloud AI and dean of Carnegie Mellon University's School of Computer Science, Lovelace's Elemental platform sits between AI agents and raw, fragmented enterprise data. It transforms this data into coherent, agent-navigable context engines, allowing both humans and AI agents to operate from a shared understanding of information. This approach also drastically reduces the number of tokens required for complex investigative tasks, potentially by a factor of 1,000, which translates into significant cost reductions for enterprises. Every conclusion generated by Elemental can be traced back to its source, providing verifiable evidence crucial for critical analysis.

name: Plaude Note Pro
description: Ultra-thin AI recorder that transcribes conversations in 112 languages and generates multidimensional summaries.
longDescription: The Plaude Note Pro is an ultra-thin AI recorder designed for transcribing conversations and generating summaries, with its features and capabilities extensively covered in various reviews and announcements around late 2025 and early 2026. Key features include: Design and Audio Capture - device is notably slim (0.117 inches thin, 1.04 ounces) with four MEMS microphones and one VPU microphone, capturing voices up to 16.4 feet away; supports smart dual-mode recording; AMOLED display shows recording status, battery life, and connection. AI Transcription and Multilingual Support - utilizes "Plaud Intelligence" powered by advanced LLMs such as GPT-5, Claude Sonnet 4, and Gemini 2.5 Pro; transcribes audio in 112 languages; offers speaker labels and custom vocabulary for enhanced accuracy. Summarization and Note-Taking - excels in generating "multidimensional summaries" from conversations using over 3,000 professional templates, mind maps, and workflow integrations; summaries can focus on key takeaways, action items, questions and answers, and decisions; features "Press to Highlight" to mark important moments during recording which are emphasized in AI-generated summaries. Companion App and Integration - operates with Plaud App (version 3.0) on iOS/Android and desktop app for PC/Mac; enables searching through transcripts, organizing recordings, exporting notes in formats like Markdown, PDF, Word, and CSV; supports multimodal input for integrating typed notes and images alongside audio. Battery and Storage - offers up to 30 hours continuous recording in enhanced mode or 50 hours in endurance mode, with standby time of 60-75 days; comes with 64 GB internal storage. The Plaude Note Pro launched on August 27, 2025, with shipping commencing in October of the same year.

name: Familiar Machines & Magic's Familiar
description: AI robot companion that communicates through nonverbal expression, body language, and behavior rather than verbal language or screens.
longDescription: Familiar Machines' Magic Familiar AI robot companion utilizes a sophisticated array of nonverbal communication methods, expressions, and body language to interact with users, according to information released in May 2026. This pet-inspired robot is designed to perceive and respond to human emotions and cues without relying on verbal communication or screens. The Magic Familiar is equipped to read facial expressions, tone of voice, and body language in real-time. Its responses are conveyed through animal-inspired body language, expressive movements, and sounds, aiming for interactions that feel natural and unscripted. For instance, the robot might offer a nuzzle when a user is stressed or wag its tail when excitement is detected. Key aspects include: Body Language and Movement - robot boasts 23 degrees of freedom enabling lifelike and expressive movements (head tilts, paw taps, high-fives, leaning into hugs, participating in activities like yoga); Perception - outfitted with vision system (camera), multiple microphones, and touch-sensitive exterior; Emotional Awareness and Adaptive Behavior - onboard AI system processes visual, audio, and memory inputs to interpret context and social cues, enabling socially responsive behaviors; robot's personality develops and adapts over time through ongoing interactions, learning household rhythms and reinforcing positive habits; Intentional Design - deliberately designed with approachable, non-humanoid, quadruped form factor optimized for expressive interaction in daily environments; communication focuses entirely on motion, behavior, and context rather than human-like speech to avoid potential misinterpretations or inappropriate factual advice.
